description: Create the item data definitions for MVP: at least 30 items across all equipment slot types with varied stats, durability, and power draw. Items are defined in a JSON data file that the equipment system reads at runtime.
acceptance_criteria: An items.json data file defines all items with fields: id, name, slot, stats (damage, armor, accuracy, visionBonus, powerDraw), durability, rarity, description.
At least 5 head-slot items exist (e.g., basic helmet, flashlight visor, sealed helmet, IR scanner, rebreather).
At least 5 torso-slot items exist (e.g., jumpsuit, light armor, power armor, hazmat suit, stealth suit).
At least 8 arm-slot items exist: 4 melee weapons (pipe, crowbar, plasma cutter, stun baton) and 4 ranged weapons (pistol, shotgun, rifle, energy weapon).
At least 4 back-slot items exist (e.g., backpack, shield generator, hacking rig, extra O2 tank).
At least 4 leg-slot items exist (e.g., standard boots, mag-boots, sprint boosters, armored greaves).
Items have a 'rarity' field (common, uncommon, rare) that affects spawn frequency in the proc-gen.
All items load correctly from the JSON file and integrate with the equipment system from STORY-0011.
spec_section: 5.2 Modular Equipment System, 11.1 MVP Scope (Modular equipment system)
notes: Spec says ~50 items for full MVP. Start with 30 for first playable. Item names from spec: 'broken pipes, severed robot arms, plasma cutter, crowbar, mag-boots.' Data-driven design means adding items later is trivial.
